forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,32 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+depends_on:
+- 01KTBN8CQDYD52W5MGG4ZYKNN7
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '8380'
+project: local-review
+title: Finding data model + structured agent-output schema
+---
+## What
+Define the structured finding type that flows through the whole pipeline — emitted by fleet agents, consumed by the verifier, and rendered by synthesis. This is the contract that makes the fleet's output machine-mergeable instead of free-text.
+
+- Create the Rust types in the new tool module `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/review/types.rs` (or a shared sibling), per the design doc `ideas/local-review.md`:
+  - `Finding { file: String, line: u32, dimension: String, severity: Severity, claim: String, evidence: String, suggestion: Option<String> }`
+  - `Severity { Blocker, Warning, Nit }` with `serde` rename to lowercase matching the existing review skill's vocabulary.
+  - A `VerifiedFinding` wrapper carrying the verifier verdict `{ confirmed: bool, reason: String }`.
+- Provide the JSON Schema (or a serde-derived example) that the fleet-agent prompt will instruct agents to emit, so an agent's raw text response parses into `Vec<Finding>`. Reuse the existing validator response parsing approach as a reference (`parse_validator_response` in `crates/avp-common/src/validator`), but this is a richer, multi-finding schema.
+- Add a parser `parse_findings(agent_text) -> Result<Vec<Finding>>` tolerant of the model wrapping JSON in prose/fences (mirror how `parse_validator_response` strips fences).
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] `Finding`, `Severity`, `VerifiedFinding` types exist with round-trip `serde` (serialize → parse) tests.
+- [ ] `Severity` serializes to exactly `blocker` / `warning` / `nit` (matches the review skill's checklist sections).
+- [ ] `parse_findings` extracts a `Vec<Finding>` from a realistic agent response that includes prose + a fenced JSON block, and returns a clear error on malformed input.
+- [ ] Field names and the dimension set match `ideas/local-review.md` exactly.
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] Unit tests: serde round-trip for each type; `parse_findings` on (a) clean JSON, (b) JSON inside ```json fences with surrounding prose, (c) malformed → `Err`.
+- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-tools review::types` green.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — write the round-trip and parser tests first. Reuse fence-stripping logic from the existing validator response parser rather than writing a new one. Depends on the design task for the exact field/dimension names.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -9,24 +9,29 @@
 title: Finding data model + structured agent-output schema
 ---
 ## What
-Define the structured finding type that flows through the whole pipeline — emitted by fleet agents, consumed by the verifier, rendered by synthesis. Lives in the engine crate `swissarmyhammer-validators` so the engine stages and the thin MCP tool both use it.
+Define the structured finding type that flows through the whole pipeline — emitted by fleet agents, consumed by the verifier, rendered by synthesis. Lives in the engine crate `swissarmyhammer-validators` (`src/review/types.rs`), per the conversation.

-- Create the Rust types in `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/types.rs`, per `ideas/local-review.md`:
-  - `Finding { file: String, line: u32, validator: String, severity: Severity, claim: String, evidence: String, suggestion: Option<String> }` — `validator` is the name of the source validator (the shard that produced it). There is NO "dimension" field.
-  - `Severity { Blocker, Warning, Nit }` with `serde` rename to lowercase matching the review skill's vocabulary.
-  - `VerifiedFinding` wrapping a `Finding` with the verifier verdict `{ confirmed: bool, reason: String }`.
-- Provide the JSON Schema (or serde example) the fleet-agent prompt instructs agents to emit so a raw response parses into `Vec<Finding>`. Reference the existing validator response parsing (`parse_validator_response`) for fence-stripping; this is a richer multi-finding schema.
-- Add `parse_findings(agent_text) -> Result<Vec<Finding>>` tolerant of prose/``` fences around the JSON.
+Types:
+- `Finding { file: String, line: u32, validator: String, rule: Option<String>, severity: Severity, claim: String, evidence: String, suggestion: Option<String> }`
+  - **`validator`** — the source validator (the shard that produced it).
+  - **`rule`** — optional: which specific rule inside the validator fired (traceability; agents cite it when known).
+  - **`claim`** — what's wrong **and why it matters**; the human-facing sentence the synthesis render uses.
+  - **`evidence`** — the *proof* the issue is real (the probe hit or code citation, e.g. "`find_duplicates`: 0.94 match at `bar.rs:88`"). Verifier/audit-facing; NOT the same as "why it matters" (that's in `claim`).
+  - **`suggestion`** — the fix (optional).
+- `Severity { Blocker, Warning, Nit }` with `serde` rename to lowercase (`blocker`/`warning`/`nit`) matching the review skill's checklist sections.
+- `VerifiedFinding { finding: Finding, confirmed: bool, reason: String }`.
+- JSON Schema (or serde example) the fleet-agent prompt instructs agents to emit so a raw response parses into `Vec<Finding>`. Reference the existing `parse_validator_response` for fence-stripping; this is a richer multi-finding schema.
+- `parse_findings(agent_text) -> Result<Vec<Finding>>` tolerant of prose/``` fences around the JSON.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `Finding`, `Severity`, `VerifiedFinding` exist in the engine crate with round-trip serde tests; `Finding` carries `validator`, not `dimension`.
+- [ ] `Finding` (with `validator`, optional `rule`, and the `claim`/`evidence`/`suggestion` semantics above), `Severity`, `VerifiedFinding` exist with round-trip serde tests.
 - [ ] `Severity` serializes to exactly `blocker` / `warning` / `nit`.
-- [ ] `parse_findings` extracts a `Vec<Finding>` from a realistic response (prose + fenced JSON) and errors clearly on malformed input.
-- [ ] Field names match `ideas/local-review.md` exactly.
+- [ ] `parse_findings` extracts a `Vec<Finding>` from a realistic response (prose + fenced JSON), tolerates a missing `rule`/`suggestion`, and errors clearly on malformed input.
+- [ ] Field names/semantics are documented on the type (claim = what+why; evidence = proof).

 ## Tests
-- [ ] Unit tests: serde round-trip per type; `parse_findings` on (a) clean JSON, (b) JSON in ```json fences with surrounding prose, (c) malformed → `Err`.
+- [ ] Unit tests: serde round-trip per type (incl. a finding with `rule: None` and one with `Some`); `parse_findings` on (a) clean JSON, (b) JSON in ```json fences with surrounding prose, (c) malformed → `Err`.
 - [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-validators review::types` green.

 ## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` — round-trip and parser tests first. Reuse fence-stripping from the existing validator response parser. Depends on the rename (engine crate name) and the design (field names).
\ No newline at end of file
+- Use `/tdd` — round-trip and parser tests first. Reuse fence-stripping from the existing validator response parser rather than writing a new one. Depends on the rename (engine crate name). (Fan-out prompt must request `rule` + the claim/evidence split — handled in the fan-out task.)
\ No newline at end of file

-  - **`evidence`** — the *proof* the issue is real (the probe hit or code citation, e.g. "`find_duplicates`: 0.94 match at `bar.rs:88`"). Verifier/audit-facing; NOT the same as "why it matters" (that's in `claim`).
-  - **`suggestion`** — the fix (optional).
-- `Severity { Blocker, Warning, Nit }` with `serde` rename to lowercase (`blocker`/`warning`/`nit`) matching the review skill's checklist sections.
-- `VerifiedFinding { finding: Finding, confirmed: bool, reason: String }`.

-- `parse_findings(agent_text) -> Result<Vec<Finding>>` tolerant of prose/``` fences around the JSON.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `Finding` (with `validator`, optional `rule`, and the `claim`/`evidence`/`suggestion` semantics above), `Severity`, `VerifiedFinding` exist with round-trip serde tests.
+- [ ] `Finding`, `Severity`, `VerifiedFinding` exist in the engine crate with round-trip serde tests; `Finding` carries `validator`, not `dimension`.
 - [ ] `Severity` serializes to exactly `blocker` / `warning` / `nit`.
-- [ ] `parse_findings` extracts a `Vec<Finding>` from a realistic response (prose + fenced JSON), tolerates a missing `rule`/`suggestion`, and errors clearly on malformed input.
-- [ ] Field names/semantics are documented on the type (claim = what+why; evidence = proof).
+- [ ] `parse_findings` extracts a `Vec<Finding>` from a realistic response (prose + fenced JSON) and errors clearly on malformed input.
+- [ ] Field names match `ideas/local-review.md` exactly.
